pr.title: Fix bad symbol and run `dtk dol apply`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated only config/GALE01/symbols.txt after fixing a bad symbol and regenerating/applying symbol metadata with `dtk dol apply`. The diff is mostly symbol-table metadata churn: scope corrections, weak/local markings, added align annotations for float/double/8-byte data, anonymous local label renumbering, and insertion of missing lbl_* data objects after jump tables. The PR had no body or review comments, so the exact original bad symbol is not identified in the available text.

key_files.role: Only changed file; GALE01 symbol map regenerated/applied with symbol scope, alignment, local-label, and data-object metadata updates.

what_changed: Changed several MetroTRK function symbols from global to local scope, including TRKSetBufferUsed, TRKValidMemory32, TRK_ppc_memcpy, TRKTargetReadInstruction, TRKExceptionHandler, TRKTargetEnableTrace, TRKTargetCheckStep, TRKLoadContext, and TRKEXICallBack.
Changed OSReport at .text:0x803456A8 from global to weak scope.

Added explicit alignment metadata to many floating-point and double data symbols, such as __constants align:8, MSL_AnsiFp_803B8BA8 align:8, tmp_float align:4, __sincos_poly align:4, gmMainLib_803D4A60 align:8, and many .data float arrays align:4.

Renamed some previously module-style or lbl_* data names to anonymous local compiler labels, for example @40 became @25 at .rodata:0x803B93A0, MTRK_TargImpl_803B93D0/803B93E4/803B93F8 became @215/@220/@225, and lbl_803C6F40/lbl_803C6F60 became @248/@249 with local scope.

Inserted missing data labels adjacent to jump tables, such as lbl_803BCC70, lbl_803C6E44, lbl_803D98C8, lbl_803DA040, lbl_803E09CC, lbl_803E3010, lbl_803E3090, lbl_803E3E34, lbl_803E5A30, and lbl_803E80C8.
Large line count change was from reapplying generated symbol metadata rather than source code edits: 5659 added and 5626 deleted lines in a single symbols.txt file.
smart_moves: After correcting a bad symbol, the author ran `dtk dol apply` so the symbol table stayed consistent with the tool-generated DOL metadata instead of making only a narrow manual edit.
The update preserved address and size information while correcting metadata such as scope and alignment, reducing the chance of downstream extraction/linker mismatches.
Explicit align annotations for float, double, and 8-byte data make symbol metadata more useful for future data declarations and matching work.
Marking internal MetroTRK routines as local and OSReport as weak captures linkage behavior that matters for accurate reconstruction.
decomp_lessons: For symbols.txt-only fixes, run the project’s DOL application/regeneration step after fixing a symbol; otherwise stale scope, alignment, and local-label metadata can remain inconsistent.
Do not assume named TRK functions are global just because they have recognizable SDK-style names; the symbol map may need local scope for internal routines.
Weak symbols should be represented explicitly in config when the original binary/link behavior requires it, as shown by OSReport changing to scope:weak.
Float and double objects should carry align metadata when the tool can infer it; future C declarations may need matching alignment to avoid data layout drift.
Anonymous local labels like @25, @215, @220, and @225 may be preferable to invented module-prefixed names when the symbol is compiler-local or lacks strong semantic evidence.
When jump tables are followed by contiguous data, make sure separate lbl_* objects are represented so symbol boundaries are not swallowed by the preceding jumptable entry.
naming_conventions: Use `scope:local` for compiler-local anonymous labels such as `@25`, `@215`, `@220`, `@225`, `@248`, and `@249`.
Use address-based fallback names like `lbl_803E3010` for unlabeled data objects where there is no semantic name evidence.
Avoid retaining semantic-looking names such as `MTRK_TargImpl_803B93D0` when the tool/application indicates the entries are local anonymous constants.
Keep generated symbol metadata format consistent: `name = .section:address; // type:... size:... scope:... align:... data:...`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `dtk dol apply` can be used as a cleanup pass after a symbol fix to propagate DOL-derived symbol metadata into config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Use scope metadata as matching evidence: local vs global vs weak can affect how functions and objects should be declared in C or headers.
Use `align:4` for float data and `align:8` for double/8-byte data when reconstructing declarations that must match emitted data layout.
Treat inserted `lbl_*` objects after jump tables as boundary hints for splitting data arrays, string blobs, or tables during future decompilation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.
Because the PR body is empty, there is no direct reviewer or author explanation identifying the exact bad symbol beyond the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Which exact bad symbol triggered PR #1514?
Find later PRs that depend on OSReport being scope:weak.
Find source declarations that should use alignas(4) or alignas(8) based on symbols.txt align metadata.
Audit MetroTRK symbols for remaining incorrect global/local scope metadata.
Search for anonymous local labels renamed by `dtk dol apply` to avoid reintroducing semantic names without evidence.
